content.problemStatement:
Let's call an array B1,B2,…,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1​,B2​,…,BM​ **good**, if Bi≠Bi+1B_i \neq B_{i+1}Bi​=Bi+1​ for any iii with 1≤i≤M−11 \le i \le M-11≤i≤M−1.

You are given a **good** array of NNN positive integers A1,A2,A3,…,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​.

You can perform the following operations on this array:


Choose any index iii (1≤i≤N)(1 \leq i \leq N)(1≤i≤N) and a number XXX (1≤X≤109)(1 \leq X \leq 10^9)(1≤X≤109). Then, set AiA_iAi​ to XXX. After this operation, the array has to remain **good**.
You want to perform several operations so that the resulting array will contain exactly two distinct values. Determine the smallest number of operations needed to achieve this goal.


Input Format


The first line of input contains the integer TTT, the number of test cases. The description of test cases follows.




The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN −-− the length of the array.




The second line of each test case contains NNN integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​  −-− elements of the array.





Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer −-− the smallest number of operations needed to achieve an array in which there are exactly two distinct values.
constraints: It's guaranteed that Ai≠Ai+1A_i \neq A_{i+1}Ai​=Ai+1​ for 1≤i≤N−11 \le i \le N-11≤i≤N−1 (that is, the array is **good**).
The sum of nnn over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
**Subtask 1 (20 points):** The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 100100100
**Subtask 2 (10 points):** The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500500500
**Subtask 3 (25 points):** The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 400040004000

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, one of the optimal sequences of operations is:
(4,5,2,4,5)→(2,5,2,4,5)→(2,5,2,4,2)→(2,5,2,5,2)(4, 5, 2, 4, 5) \to (2, 5, 2, 4, 5) \to (2, 5, 2, 4, 2) \to (2, 5, 2, 5, 2)(4,5,2,4,5)→(2,5,2,4,5)→(2,5,2,4,2)→(2,5,2,5,2).
In the second test case, the array already contains only two distinct values, so the answer is 000.
